footnotes: {@sup *} This is the 'off-the-shelf' purchase cost and can vary significantly from the crafting cost.

monster.name: Prince Jorfraust

trait.name: Ferrous Weakness
entries: When Jorfraust takes 4 or more bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage in a single instance from a nonmagical weapon made with iron, he has disadvantage on the next ability check, saving throw, or attack roll he makes before the end of his next turn.

trait.name: Ice Walk
entries: Jorfraust can move across and climb icy surfaces without needing to make an ability check and automatically succeeds on saving throws made to avoid falling {@condition prone} due to an icy surface. Additionally, difficult terrain composed of ice or snow doesn't cost him extra movement.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (2/Day)
entries: If Jorfraust fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: Jorfraust's weapon attacks are magical and deal an extra 2 ({@damage 1d4}) cold damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Winter's Heart (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: When Jorfraust would be reduced to 0 hit points, his current hit point total instead resets to 97 hit points, he recharges his Cold Snap, and he regains 1 expended use of Legendary Resistance. A violent blizzard begins to build in the sky within 1 mile of Jorfraust. The affected area is lightly obscured and the gale-force winds halve the speed of other creatures in the area. Additionally, Jorfraust can now use the options in the "Mythic Actions" section for 1 hour. Award a party an additional 1,100 XP (2,200 XP total) for defeating Jorfraust after his Winter's Heart activates.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Jorfraust makes two attacks with any combination of his Claw and Icicle.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +5} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) slashing damage plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) cold damage.

action.name: Icicle
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit +5} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) piercing damage plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) cold damage.

action.name: Endoleech
entries: {@atk ms} {@hit +4} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 3d6}) cold damage and, until the end of target's next turn, it's speed is reduced by 15 feet and Jorfraust's speed is increased by 15 feet.

action.name: Cold Snap {@recharge 5}
entries: Jorfraust snaps his fingers and a burst of freezing energy explodes from him in a 10-foot sphere. Creatures within the area must make a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw. A creature that fails the save begins to turn to ice and is {@condition restrained}. The {@condition restrained} creature must repeat the saving throw at the end of its next turn, becoming {@condition petrified} on a failure or ending the effect on a success. A creature {@condition petrified} in this way gains 3 temporary hit points and is immune to all damage except fire damage. When these temporary hit points are lost, the creature is freed from its ice prison and is no longer {@condition petrified}. A creature that succeeds on its saving throw is immune to Jorfraust's Cold Snap for 24 hours.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: Jorfraust makes one Claw or Icicle attack.

legendary.name: Ice Skate
entries: Jorfraust sprays up snow and ice before darting away; he moves up to half his speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendary.name: Melancholy
entries: Jorfraust unleashes a deluge of sorrow against a creature he can see within 60 feet of him. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 12} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} until the end of Jorfraust's next turn. Creatures with the fey type have disadvantage on this saving throw. A creature that is {@condition charmed} in this way has its speed halved, suffers a -2 penalty to its AC, has disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws, can't use reactions, and can only use an action or bonus action on their turn, not both.

mythicHeader: If Jorfraust's Winter's Heart trait has activated in the last hour, he can use the options below as legendary actions.

mythic.name: Lunging Strike
entries: Jorfraust moves up to half his speed without provoking opportunity attacks and makes one attack with his Claw.

mythic.name: Winter's Charge
entries: Jorfraust channels shards of ice across his body and blasts forward in a sudden explosion of movement. Jorfraust moves in a straight line up to his movement speed. Each creature whose space he moves through during this movement must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or take 3 ({@damage 1d6}) slashing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) cold damage and be knocked {@condition prone}.

mythic.name: Ice Mirage (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Jorfraust casts {@spell mirror image}. When a duplicate is reduced to 0 hit points, it shatters into jagged shards of ice in a 10-foot-radius. Creatures in the affected area must make a {@dc 12} Dexterity saving throw, taking 3 ({@damage 1d6}) cold damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

headerEntries: The queen's innate spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save {@dc 14}, {@hit +6} to hit with spell attacks). Cantrips are cast as an 8th-level caster. She can innately cast the following spells, requiring no material components:

2e: {@spell barkskin} (the queen casts this spell on herself before combat)

trait.name: Ferrous Weakness
entries: When the queen takes 4 or more bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage on a hit from a nonmagical weapon made with iron, she has disadvantage on the next ability check, saving throw, or attack roll she makes before the end of her next turn.

entries: If the Queen fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead.

entries: When the queen would be reduced to 0 hit points, her current hit point total instead resets to 58 hit points, and she recharges her Thorn Barrage. A pair of bat-like shadow wings sprouts from the queen's back and she gains a flying speed of 60 feet. Award a party an additional 1,800 XP (3,600 XP total) for defeating the queen after her Shadow Soul activates.

trait.name: Soul Shield
entries: While the Queen of Brambles is within 10 feet of Shael, she is surrounded by a necrotic aura. A creature that touches the queen or hits her with a melee attack while within 10 feet of her takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) necrotic damage.

entries: The queen's weapon attacks are magical and deal an extra 2 ({@damage 1d4}) necrotic damage on a hit (included in the attack).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Queen casts a spell or uses Fey Charm. She also makes two Sickle attacks.

action.name: Sickle
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit +3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) slashing damage plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) necrotic damage. The target must succeed on a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute.

action.name: Fey Charm
entries: The queen targets one humanoid or beast that she can see within 30 feet of her. If the target can see the queen, it must succeed on a {@dc 14} Wisdom saving throw or be magically {@condition charmed} by the Queen. The {@condition charmed} creature regards the Queen as a trusted friend to be heeded and protected. entries: A creature that touches the bramblekin or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes half {@damage 2d4;3d6;4d6|Vdam} piercing damage.

items.name: Ferrous Weakness

monsterFluff.name: Prince Jorfraust

entries.name: Winter Fey
entries: Though winter fey are melancholic creatures, they are not lethargic or lifeless. They see the sorrow of the world with enhanced acuity and seek others with whom to share their grief. Few are capable of being in the presence of such a creature for long, and the ensuing loneliness such fey experience only acts to worsen their sorrow.

entries.name: Empathic Fey
entries: The more powerful a fey creature, the more influence its emotion has on the world around it. For fey creatures that are not of the winter variety, a winter fey's desolation can be stifling, inducing a torpor-like inactivity. Fey not used to this oppressive sadness may find themselves unable to have the motivation to feed themselves, wasting away to nothing, and hence winter fey often find themselves marginalised from the wider community.

entries.name: Base Iron
entries: According to the laws of arcanomagnetism, like forces repel. Some winter fey are so magical that they innately repel attacks made with magical weapons, much as positively-charged magnets repel one another. Unfortunately, this powerful resistance has a counterpart vulnerability; contact with base iron and mundane steel can cause a poison-like effect, severely debilitating these powerful creatures for a short time. This goes some way to explain why only minor fey tend to venture into the Material Plane; the more powerful amongst them can be easily crippled by the spoon with which they sup their soup.

As Jorfraust grew stronger, his fits of dejection caused blizzards to roam through the halls of the Sunsworn Court. Unable to discern his origin, Queen Soliana could not fathom the heights to which his power might grow. Rather than foist the child on an unsuspecting neighbour, the queen took the uneasy decision to have her closest advisor, Mildred, enchant a vessel to contain the young prince and his emotions. For decades, the prince has wiled away in his frozen castle, feeding himself on the frostamanders that magically spawn in its gloomy crevices.

monsterFluff.name: Frosty

entries: If you really vant to brink ein frosty into ze tavern, have ze decency to be discreet. Lutecia is still sore zat L'Arsène prefers ein snowman's buttered rum to her own, und I kan't handle another snowball fight over zis. I am still recoverink from ze last...

When the spirit of a winter fey is magically infused into a pile of snow, the result is a frosty: a happy little elemental full of unique idiosyncrasies. In stark contrast to the harsh cold of winter, frosties embody the more pleasant aspects of their season, seeking to spread warmth through their jovial attitudes, community spirit, and of course, steamy beverages.
Common quirks of frosties include a passion for accessorising—in particular, donning different hats, scarves, and pipes—and a strange distrust of dogs.
If any of the frosty's traits or actions require a saving throw, it is always against the frosty's save DC, as calculated below.

entries.name: Save DC
entries.text: 8 + the tamer's proficiency bonus + the frosty's Charisma modifier

entries.name: Monster Trainer
entries: In addition to the improvements you can normally give your companions, you can choose to grant the redwing the following improvements using your Monster Trainer feature:

entries.name: Growth I
Prerequisite:
entries: Become a tamer's companion.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).

The frosty's size increases to Small, its Hit Die size increases to a d6 (its hit point maximum consequently increases by 1 for each of its Hit Dice), and the damage die of its Horns attack increases to a d6.

entries.name: Snowballs
Prerequisite:
entries: 3rd-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).
The frosty gains a ranged weapon attack: Snowball. The damage die of the Snowball attack increases with the die size specified in the frosty's Growth II improvement.
{@b Snowball.} Ranged Weapon Attack: Str modifier + PB to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 1d6 + Str modifier cold damage.

entries.name: Winter Drinks
Prerequisite:
entries: 3rd-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).

The frosty carries a small keg filled with all sorts of wonderful drinks. As an action, it can pour one of the options below (tamer's choice) into a mug and immediately feed it to a willing creature within 5 feet of it, which gains its effects for 1 minute.

items: {@b Soothing Chai.} The creature is immune to being {@condition frightened}.
{@b Hot Cocoa.} The creature has resistance to cold damage.
{@b Warm Cider.} The creature is immune to being {@condition poisoned}.

entries: Once the frosty takes this action, it can't take it again until its tamer finishes a short or long rest.

entries.name: Growth II
Prerequisite:
entries: 5th-level tamer, Growth I.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).

The frosty's size increases to Medium, its Hit Die size increases to a d8 (its hit point maximum consequently increases by 1 for each of its Hit Dice), and the damage die of its attacks increases to a d8.

entries.name: Liquefaction
Prerequisite:
entries: 5th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (reaction).

When the frosty is hit by an attack that doesn't deal fire damage, it can use its reaction to selectively melt a portion of its body and soften the blow, halving the attack's damage against it and suffering the effects of its Fire Susceptibility trait as if it took fire damage.

Once the frosty uses this reaction, it can't use it again until its tamer finishes a short or long rest. When the frosty's tamer reaches 13th level in the tamer class, the frosty can use this reaction twice, regaining all expended uses when its tamer finishes a short or long rest.

entries.name: Multiattack
Prerequisite:
entries: 5th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).
As an action, the frosty can make two attacks.

entries.name: Holiday Spirits
Prerequisite:
entries: 9th-level tamer, Winter Drinks.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).
The frosty adds a few alcoholic beverages to its selection. When it feeds a creature a drink with its Warm Drinks improvement, its tamer can also choose between the following additional options:

items: {@b Buttered Rum.} The creature's speed is increased by 10 feet.
{@b Creamy Eggnog.} The creature gains 7 ({@damage 2d6}) temporary hit points at the start of each of its turns.
{@b Mulled Wine.} The creature's attacks deal an extra 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage on a hit.

entries: In addition, the frosty can use its Warm Drinks feature twice, regaining all uses when its tamer finishes a short or long rest.

entries.name: Iceballs
Prerequisite:
entries: 9th-level tamer, Snowballs.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (bonus action).

As a bonus action, the frosty can compact a few snowballs especially tightly and freeze them into hard ice. Until the end of its turn, its Snowball attacks deal an extra 7 ({@damage 2d6}) bludgeoning damage on a hit.

Once the frosty has used this bonus action, it can't do so again until its tamer finishes a short or long rest. When the frosty's tamer reaches 13th level in the tamer class, the extra damage increases to 9 ({@damage 2d8}).

entries.name: Brewbomb
Prerequisite:
entries: 13th-level tamer, Holiday Spirits.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).

As an action, the frosty can spend two uses of its Warm Drinks feature to create a volatile concoction and lob it at a point within 30 feet of it, where it detonates in a 20-foot radius sphere of scalding hot liquid. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 27 ({@damage 6d8}) fire damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and isn't knocked {@condition prone}.

entries.name: Cold Blooded
Prerequisite:
entries: 13th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Passive (companion).
The frosty gains immunity to cold damage. When it is subjected to cold damage, it gains temporary hit points equal to the cold damage dealt, which last for 1 minute.

entries.name: Mixology
Prerequisite:
entries: 13th-level tamer, Holiday Spirits.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).

When the frosty uses its Warm Drinks feature, it can spend two uses of it to mix two beverages together. Choose one drink from the Warm Drinks options and one from the Holiday Spirits options. The creature that drinks this cocktail gains the effects of both choices, which last for 1 hour instead of 1 minute.

entries.name: Snow Fort
Prerequisite:
entries: 17th-level tamer.

entries.name: Type
entries: Active (action).
The frosty can use an action to cast the {@spell wall of ice} spell. Once the frosty has used this action, it can't do so again until its tamer finishes a long rest.

entries.name: The Queen of Brambles
entries: Born as the Sunsworn Princess, the Queen of Brambles' soul was severed from her juvenile body and banished to the Plane of Shadow. As with all creatures that enter the Plane of Shadow, her physical form began to change. Her skin paled until it was as colourless as the moon, her curls straightened and fell flat and shapeless down her back, and her plump spring form wasted away until a long and lithe creature remained.

Taken in by a night hag, the Queen of Brambles learnt soul magic and raised a horde of thorny creatures to serve as her Court of Brambles. She is a cunning and powerful leader, feared by many and adored by her people. Her desire to reunite with the other half of her soul has driven her to great lengths, and she will stop at nothing to achieve her goal.

entries.name: Shael, the Sunsworn Princess
entries: Shael, the Princess of the Sunsworn Court, is a radiant vision of fey beauty with a curvaceous form that appears to glow with the power of the sun. Her long hair flows in waves of golden light, and her piercing blue eyes seem to peer directly into one's soul. Outwardly a kind and generous person, Shael has an inner strength of will reminiscent of the most hardened veteran.

The princess lived a life of luxury in the faerie court under the watchful eyes of her mother, Queen Soliana, and in the arms of her lover, the hot-headed summer fey Iris Clearwind. Raised as heir to the Sunsworn Court, Shael was unaware that half her soul was severed from her body and banished to the Plane of Shadow by her own mother. Once the secret was revealed, she chose to follow the Queen of Brambles into the shadow realm in an attempt to reunite their souls once more.

monsterFluff.name: Iris Clearwind
entries: Iris Clearwind is a summer fey with a muscular yet elegant frame. Long hours spent training with a blade are evident in the balance of her stance and the way her fierce, ruby eyes quickly assess newcomers for threats. She has been secretly courting Princess Shael, and even saving the realm comes second to her desire to rescue her beloved.

entries.name: Iris Clearwind
entries: {@i Fey, knight, she/her}

items: {@b Personality.} Fiery, impulsive, and fiercely loyal. She admits when she is wrong and is quick to make amends.
{@b Appearance.} Slender build, long red hair, and ruby-red eyes. Her clothes are typically crimson and gold.
{@b Desire.} To raise younglings in the safe spring of the Sunsworn Court.
{@b Fear.} That she will find herself masterless and without purpose.

entries: I would risk everything to protect those I love, no matter the cost.

monsterFluff.name: Frostfire Phoenix
entries: A frostfire phoenix is a majestic bird of prey with a 3-foot wingspan of shimmering feathers made of frozen flames. These exquisite creatures have piercing sapphire eyes and a fierce, regal bearing. Frostfire phoenixes are known for their ability to burst into flames when they die, melting their current forms and being reborn from the resulting puddle.

monsterFluff.name: Mildred Magpie
entries: Mildred Magpie is a hunched autumn hag with a wild mane of curly, nest-like hair that moves like it has a life of its own. She has pale green skin stretched taut over old bones, and her eyes shift through autumn hues based on her mood. Despite Mildred's dishevelled appearance, she is a master enchantress, able to weave powerful spells and curses easily. She serves as the right-hand advisor to Queen Soliana, always looking out for the best interests of the Sunsworn court.

entries.name: Mildred Magpie
entries: {@i Fey, autumn hag (uses green hag statistics), she/her}

items: {@b Personality.} Sharp-tongued, but kind-hearted and highly intelligent. Deeply loyal to the queen and court.
{@b Appearance.} Angular features, hunched frame, and long, wild hair. Dresses in simple robes in autumn colours.
{@b Desire.} To prove that not all hags are to be mistrusted and reviled.
{@b Fear.} To lose herself to the shadow like her sisters who turned into night hags.

entries: The truth may be hard to hear, but it is essential for our survival.

entries.name: Frostfall Defenders
entries: Various creatures adapted to the cold have flocked to Jorfraust's banner and now call the Frostfall Kingdom home. While many roam the taiga and hunt down the former residents, others have gathered in the Sunsworn Courtyard to defend their king. These creatures would not typically be found living side by side, and only the promise of this new kingdom keeps them allied under a single purpose—they fight to the death to defend their new home.

monsterFluff.name: Shadowstalker
entries: Shadowstalkers are tiny feline creatures with bat-like wings and silvery horns. While they are notoriously difficult to capture, it is possible to domesticate them as familiars. Their ability to fly and turn invisible makes them valued by the residents for hunting and scouting.

monsterFluff.name: Wrabit
entries: Wrabit has been tasked with telling the bramblekin residents to attend the court for a royal ceremony. Wrabit knows the ceremony takes place tomorrow and is the joining of the Queen of Brambles and the Sunsworn Princess. A successful {@dc 15} Charisma ({@skill Persuasion} or {@skill Intimidation}) check convinces Wrabit to share that information. On a failed check, it turns invisible and flees.

entries.name: Bramble Soldiers
entries: A bramble soldier uses the statistics referenced in each wave with the following modifications:

items: It is immune to poison damage.
It is immune to the {@condition charmed} and {@condition poisoned} conditions.
It has the Thorny Defence and Ferrous Weakness traits.

legendaryGroup.name: Winter Fey
lairActions: Jorfraust's presence has created a winter vortex of fey magic that constantly ebbs and flows. This ambient magic creates effects that can be used as lair actions within the courtyard. The saving throw DC and damage of the lair actions depend on the level of the hunt, as shown in the Variable Lair Action Statistics table.
On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), a lair action is taken; the same effect can't be used two rounds in a row:

items.name: Hoarfrost
entries: The temperature drops rapidly and freezing wind blasts through the lair. Each creature in the lair must succeed on a {@footnote VDC|12/14/16} Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 1d6;2d6;4d6|Vdam} cold damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. A creature that fails the saving throw by 5 or more gains 1 level of {@condition exhaustion}. A creature with immunity to cold damage automatically succeeds on this saving throw.

items.name: Melancholic Breeze
entries: A wave of melancholy energy fills the entire lair. Each non-winter-fey creature within the lair must succeed on a {@footnote VDC|12/14/16} Wisdom saving throw or suffer a {@dice 1d4} penalty to ability checks, saving throws, and attack rolls it makes until initiative count 20 on the next round. Fey creatures make this saving throw with disadvantage.

regionalEffects: Jorfraust's wintery power has extended over the area surrounding the Sunsworn Court for miles, creating one or more of the following effects in the Frostfall Kingdom:

items.name: Eternal Winter
entries: The temperature has dropped far below freezing within 10 miles of the Sunsworn Court. Creatures travelling through the area must succeed on a {@footnote VDC|12/14/16} Constitution saving throw at the end of each hour spent in the cold or suffer 1 level of {@condition exhaustion}. Creatures with resistance or immunity to cold damage automatically succeed on the saving throw, as do creatures wearing cold weather gear and creatures adapted to cold climates. In addition, the entire region's outdoor areas outside of the Sunsworn Palace is difficult terrain due to the deep snow and treacherous ice.

items.name: Sinkholes
entries: Heavy snowfall has accumulated into unstable drifts within 5 miles of the Sunsworn Court. Any creature moving through a drift must succeed on a {@footnote VDC|12/14/16} Dexterity saving throw or fall into a sinkhole, becoming {@condition restrained} in the snow. A creature can use an action to make a {@footnote VDC|12/14/16} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check, freeing itself or another {@condition restrained} creature within its reach on a success.

items.name: Flash-freeze
entries: The area within 1 mile of the Sunsworn Court is so cold that liquids freeze solid almost instantly when exposed to the air.

regionalEffects: If Jorfraust is defeated, these effects fade over the next {@b 24 hours}.

legendaryGroup.name: The Split Souls
lairActions: The Queen of Bramble's magic has seeped into the court and become an extension of her will. This ambient magic creates effects that can be made as lair actions within the courtyard. The saving throw DC and damage of the lair actions depend on the level of the hunt, as shown in the Variable Lair Action Statistics table.
On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the Queen of Brambles takes a lair action; the same effect cannot be used two rounds in a row:

items.name: Thorny Barrage
entries: A barrage of thorns rains down from the ceiling in a 15-foot-radius circle within the lair. Creatures within the area must succeed on a {@footnote VDC|13/15/16} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d4;3d6;4d6|Vdam} piercing damage and be {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Ensnaring Roots
entries: Grasping roots shoot up from the ground 15-foot-radius circle centred on a point the Queen of Brambles chooses within 60 feet of her. Creatures within the area must make a {@footnote VDC|13/15/16} Dexterity saving throw or be {@condition restrained} until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Bramble Wall
entries: Thorny brambles sprout from the floor, creating a 20-foot long, 10-foot high, and 5-foot thick wall. The wall lasts for 1 minute and provides total cover for creatures behind it. Creatures moving through the wall must make a {@footnote VDC|13/15/16} Dexterity saving throw, taking Vdam x 2 piercing damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. Any 5-foot cube of wall exposed to fire burns away in 2 rounds, dealing {@damage 2d4;3d6;4d6|Vdam} fire damage to any creature that starts its turn in the fire.

regionalEffects: The Queen of Brambles' magic has extended over the area surrounding her court for miles, creating one or more of the following effects:

items.name: Darkening
entries: The darkness within a 1-mile radius surrounding the Court of Brambles is so complete that creatures with darkvision have their vision range reduced by half.

items.name: Soul Drain
entries: The negative energy within a 5-mile radius surrounding the Court of Brambles drains the life force of creatures who spend too much time there. At the end of each hour a creature spends within this area must succeed on a {@footnote VDC|13/15/16} Constitution saving throw or gain 1 level of {@condition exhaustion}. Undead and creatures acclimated to the Plane of Shadow automatically succeed on the saving throw.

items.name: Awakened Plantlife
entries: Gnarled trees and thorn-covered vines overgrow the area within a 10-mile radius surrounding the Court of Brambles. Many of these plants are sentient and loyal to the Queen of Brambles, acting as her eyes and ears throughout the region.

regionalEffects: If the Queen of Brambles is defeated, these effects fade over the next {@dice 1d10} days.

spell.name: Freezing Beam
entries: An invisible beam of subzero, draining cold rushes out from you in a 5-foot-wide, 100-foot-long line in a direction you choose. Each creature in the line must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 6d6} cold damage and its speed is halved until the end of its next turn. On a successful saving throw, a creature takes half as much damage and its speed isn't reduced.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each slot level above 3rd.

spell.name: Teeth of Ice
entries: Glistening lances of ice plummet from above at 3 different points you can see within range. Each creature in a 10-foot radius centred on each point must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes {@damage 6d6} cold damage and {@damage 6d6} piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. In addition, until the end of your next turn, each area is covered in jagged ice spikes, causing it to be difficult terrain; when a creature moves into or within the area, it takes {@damage 3d6} piercing damage for every 5 feet it travels.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 8th level or higher, the damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each slot level above 7th.

spell.name: Enrage
entries: You attempt to create violent emotions in a group of people. Each humanoid in a 20-foot-radius sphere centred on a point you choose within range must make a Charisma saving throw; a creature can choose to fail this saving throw if it wishes. A creature that fails this saving throw becomes enraged for the duration.

While enraged, a target becomes hostile towards all creatures. An enraged creature that is concentrating on a spell must succeed on a DC 10 Constitution saving throw at the start of each of its turns or lose concentration on the spell.

As an action, you can force an enraged creature to use its reaction to make one melee weapon attack against one randomly determined creature within the enraged creature's reach. If the enraged creature was friendly towards the target of its attack before this spell was cast, the enraged creature can repeat the saving throw immediately after making the attack, ending the effect on itself on a success.

An enraged creature can repeat the saving throw whenever it takes damage, ending the effect on itself on a success. When the spell ends, the creature reverts to its previous disposition again, unless the GM rules otherwise.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher and use an action to force an enraged creature to make a melee weapon attack, you can force one additional enraged creature to make a melee weapon attack for each slot level above 3rd.

spell.name: Pins & Needles
entries: You momentarily shut down a small part of the nervous system of a creature you can see within range, before overstimulating it and causing crippling pain. The creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 1d8} psychic damage. If the target fails its saving throw by 5 or more, it has disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn as it temporarily loses full control of a part of its body.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: This spell's damage increases by {@dice 1d8} when you reach 5th level ({@dice 2d8}), 11th level ({@dice 3d8}), and 17th level ({@dice 4d8}).

spell.name: Mortiferous Pulse
entries: You summon a bolt of negative energy to stimulate and transmit necrotic pathogens into those that stand before you, decaying their internal viscera. Each creature in a 5-foot-wide, 50-foot-long line extending from you must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 3d8} necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each slot level above 2nd.

spell.name: Soul Rend
entries: You attempt to wrench the soul of a creature you can see within range from its corporeal form, forcing it to make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes {@damage 4d6} psychic damage and {@damage 4d6} necrotic damage, and is dazed until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half as much damage and isn't dazed. While dazed, a creature can't take reactions, can take only an action or bonus action on its turn, not both, and has its speed halved.
A creature that is reduced to 0 hit points in this way dies and can't be revived by a {@spell revivify} spell. Creatures without souls are unaffected by this spell.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, both damage types increase by {@dice 1d6} for each slot level above 4th.
